---
title: "esr"
output: html_document
date: "2025-10-22"
---


```{r}
 library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
Blood_count <- read_excel("Blood_count.xlsx")
```

```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `total_cell` = `[Cells] c/ml`
  )
```

```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Shark_Id` = `Sharks-Id`
  )
```

```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    esr        = `ESR (mm/h)`
  )
```

```{r}
Blood_data_clean <- Blood_count %>%
  select(
    Groupe,
    Shark_Id,
    Sex,
    week,
    total_cell, 
    Eggs,
    esr
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(total_cell) & 
    !is.na(Groupe) & 
    !is.na(week)
)
```

```{r}
cat("Original rows:", nrow(Blood_count), "\n")
cat("Cleaned rows:", nrow(Blood_data_clean), "\n")
head(Blood_data_clean)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Create the new binary factor: 'No' (0) vs. 'Yes' (1 or 2)
Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    # Map the specific numeric Eggs values to a factor
    Egg_State_Binary = case_when(
      Eggs %in% c(1, 2) ~ "Yes",  # 1 or 2 = Eggs present/developed
      Eggs == 0 ~ "No",           # 0 = No eggs (just laid)
      TRUE ~ NA_character_        # Preserves all other values (like the reference level, or NA for males) as NA
    ),
    # Convert the new column to a factor, setting "No" as the reference level
    Egg_State_Binary = factor(Egg_State_Binary, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
  )

```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# --- 0. Data Cleaning ---
# Fix French label (Assuming Blood_data_clean is the current working dataframe name)
Blood_data_clean$Groupe[Blood_data_clean$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Control"

# Convert to numeric and factors
Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  mutate(
    esr_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(esr)),
    # Create the descriptive factor for weeks for better labels
    week_label = factor(
      paste("Week", week), 
      levels = c("Week 4", "Week 6", "Week 8") # Ensure the order is correct
    ),
    # Explicitly set factor levels 
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Control", "Winter", "Summer"))
  )

# --- 1. Remove NA ESR values ---
Blood_data_esr_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(esr_numeric))

# --- 2. Define custom colors (Using the Antibody plot's palette) ---
antibody_colors <- c(
  "Control" = "#D6C9AE", # Antibody Control color
  "Winter"  = "#6F9FCC", # Antibody Winter color
  "Summer"  = "#D97A66"  # Antibody Summer color
)

# --- 3. Create Cleaned Boxplot ---
boxplot_grouped_esr_final <- ggplot(
  Blood_data_esr_clean, 
  aes(x = Groupe_factor, y = esr_numeric, fill = Groupe_factor) # X-axis is Group
) +
  # Use geom_boxplot
  geom_boxplot(
    # Use solid black dots for outliers
    outlier.shape = 19, 
    outlier.colour = "black", 
    outlier.size = 2
  ) +
  
  # --- Facet by Descriptive Week Label, moved to the BOTTOM ---
  facet_wrap(
    ~ week_label, 
    scales = "fixed", 
    strip.position = "bottom" # Moves the labels below the panels
  ) +
  
  # Apply the Antibody Colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = antibody_colors) + # CRITICAL FIX: Use the new color set
  
  # Labels and Theme Adjustments
  labs(
    title = "", # Removed Plot Title
    x = "", # Removed X-axis label
    y = "ESR Value",
    fill = "" # Removed Legend Title
  ) +
  
  # Use a clean theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
        # Remove the x-axis labels (Control, Summer, Winter)
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        
        # Adjust facet strip to be at the bottom and clean up background
        strip.placement = "outside",
        strip.background = element_blank(), 
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12), 
        
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines")
  )

print(boxplot_grouped_esr_final)
```
```{r}
library(car)  # Needed for leveneTest

# Levene's test for homogeneity of variances across groups and weeks
levene_result_esr <- leveneTest(esr_numeric ~ Groupe * week, 
                                data = Blood_data_esr_clean)
print(levene_result_esr)

```
```{r}
shapiro_result <- shapiro.test(Blood_data_esr_clean$esr_numeric)

print(shapiro_result)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# --- 0. Data Cleaning and Preparation (Re-run to ensure consistency) ---
# We use the 'Blood_data_clean' object and create the necessary factored/numeric columns.
Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  # 1. Ensure a clean numeric ESR column exists
  mutate(esr_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(esr))) %>%
  # 2. Ensure 'Groupe' and 'week' are factors
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )

# 3. Create a filtered dataset containing only valid ESR data
Blood_data_esr_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(esr_numeric)) 

# --- 1. Create the QQ Plot for ESR ---
qq_plots_esr <- ggplot(Blood_data_esr_clean, aes(sample = esr_numeric)) +
  
  # Create a separate panel for every unique Groupe/week combination
  # Note: The facet_grid syntax here is Y_variable ~ X_variable
  facet_grid(Groupe ~ week) +
  
  # Add the plot points and the normality line
  stat_qq() + 
  stat_qq_line() +
  
  # Customize labels and theme
  labs(
    title = "QQ Plots for ESR Value: Normality Check by Groupe and Week",
    x = "Theoretical Quantiles",
    y = "Sample Quantiles (ESR Value)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

# --- 2. Display the plot ---
print(qq_plots_esr)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)

# --- 0. Data Cleaning and Preparation ---

Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  # 1. Create a clean numeric ESR column
  mutate(esr_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(esr))) %>%
  # 2. Ensure 'Groupe' and 'week' are factors
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )

# 3. Filter out rows with NA in the numeric ESR data
Blood_data_esr_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(esr_numeric)) 

# --- 1. Define and Apply Safe Shapiro-Wilk Test ---

# Function to safely extract Shapiro-Wilk results
safe_shapiro_test <- function(data) {
  n <- length(data)
  
  # Shapiro-Wilk test requires between 3 and 5000 samples
  if (n >= 3 && n <= 5000) {
    # Check for zero variance (test fails if all values are identical)
    if (var(data, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) {
      return(tibble(n = n, shapiro_w = NA_real_, shapiro_p = NA_real_, note = "Zero variance"))
    }
    
    # Perform the test
    test_result <- shapiro.test(data)
    return(tibble(
      n = n,
      shapiro_w = test_result$statistic,
      shapiro_p = test_result$p.value,
      note = NA_character_
    ))
  } else {
    return(tibble(n = n, shapiro_w = NA_real_, shapiro_p = NA_real_, note = "Sample size issue"))
  }
}

# Apply the test across all subgroups (Groupe by week)
shapiro_results <- Blood_data_esr_clean %>%
  group_by(Groupe, week) %>%
  do(safe_shapiro_test(.$esr_numeric)) %>%
  ungroup()


print(shapiro_results)

```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)

# --- 0. Data Cleaning and Preparation (Finalized) ---

Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  # Create the numeric ESR column
  mutate(esr_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(esr))) %>%
  # Ensure 'Groupe' and 'week' are factors
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )

# Filter out NA ESR values AND groups/weeks that you know are incomplete
Blood_data_esr_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(esr_numeric)) %>%
  # Explicitly filter out the incomplete subgroups to balance the design
  filter(!(Groupe == "Winter" & week == "4")) %>%
  filter(!(Groupe == "Contrôle" & week == "4"))

# --- 1. Run the Two-Way ANOVA ---

# The formula checks for main effects of Groupe, week, and their interaction.
anova_model <- aov(esr_numeric ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_data_esr_clean)

# --- 2. Display the ANOVA Summary Table ---

# The summary shows the Sum of Squares, F-statistic, and p-value for each factor.
summary(anova_model)
```
```{r}
# Load the required package for Tukey's HSD
# The 'TukeyHSD' function is part of the base 'stats' package, but we'll use 'aov' from 'stats'
# We'll re-run the aov model here for completeness, though it uses the 'stats' package, not tidyverse.

# --- Re-run the ANOVA Model (Ensure the model object is available) ---
# Assuming Blood_data_esr_clean is the correct filtered dataset and the model was:
# anova_model <- aov(esr_numeric ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_data_esr_clean)

# --- 1. Tukey's HSD for the 'Groupe' Factor ---
# This compares the overall mean ESR of each group (Contrôle vs Summer, etc.)
tukey_groupe <- TukeyHSD(anova_model, which = "Groupe")
print("--- Tukey's HSD Results for GROUPE ---")
print(tukey_groupe)

# --- 2. Tukey's HSD for the 'week' Factor ---
# This compares the overall mean ESR for each week (Week 4 vs 6, etc.)
tukey_week <- TukeyHSD(anova_model, which = "week")
print("\n--- Tukey's HSD Results for WEEK ---")
print(tukey_week)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)

# --- 0. Data Preparation (Filter for Summer) ---

# Re-use the clean data, ensuring the 'esr_numeric' and 'week' columns are ready
Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  mutate(esr_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(esr))) %>%
  mutate(week = factor(week)) 

# Create a new dataset containing ONLY the Summer group with valid ESR data
summer_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(esr_numeric)) %>%
  filter(Groupe == "Summer")

# --- 1. Run the One-Way ANOVA (Effect of 'week' on Summer Group) ---

# The formula checks for the main effect of 'week' within the filtered data.
anova_summer_model <- aov(esr_numeric ~ week, data = summer_data_clean)

# --- 2. Display the ANOVA Summary Table ---

summary(anova_summer_model)
```
```{r}
# Only run this if the One-Way ANOVA p-value for 'week' is <= 0.05

print("--- Post-hoc Tukey's HSD for Summer Group Weeks ---")
TukeyHSD(anova_summer_model, which = "week")
```
```{r}
# Ensure numeric column
Blood_data_clean$esr <- as.numeric(Blood_data_clean$esr)

# Calculate mean
mean_esr <- aggregate(esr ~ Groupe + week, data = Blood_data_clean, FUN = mean)

# Calculate SD
sd_esr <- aggregate(esr ~ Groupe + week, data = Blood_data_clean, FUN = sd)

# Combine into one summary dataframe
ESR_summary <- merge(mean_esr, sd_esr, by = c("Groupe", "week"))
colnames(ESR_summary) <- c("Groupe", "week", "mean_esr", "sd_esr")

# Inspect
print(ESR_summary)
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(ESR_summary, aes(x = factor(week), y = mean_esr, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_esr - sd_esr, ymax = mean_esr + sd_esr),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "Mean ESR per Group Across Weeks",
       x = "Week",
       y = "ESR") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)

# Filter for Summer only and weeks 4, 6, 8
summer_data <- Blood_data_esr_clean %>%
  filter(Groupe == "Summer", week %in% c("4", "6", "8"))

# Kruskal-Wallis test across weeks
kruskal_summer <- kruskal_test(summer_data, esr_numeric ~ week)
print(kruskal_summer)

# Post-hoc pairwise Wilcoxon tests between weeks
pairwise_summer <- pairwise_wilcox_test(
  summer_data,
  esr_numeric ~ week,
  p.adjust.method = "BH"
)
print(pairwise_summer)
```

```{r}
week6_data <- Blood_data_esr_clean %>%
  filter(week == "6")

kruskal_week6 <- kruskal_test(week6_data, esr_numeric ~ Groupe)
pairwise_week6 <- pairwise_wilcox_test(
  week6_data,
  esr_numeric ~ Groupe,
  p.adjust.method = "BH"
)

print(kruskal_week6)
print(pairwise_week6)

# --- Compare groups at week 8 ---
week8_data <- Blood_data_esr_clean %>%
  filter(week == "8")

kruskal_week8 <- kruskal_test(week8_data, esr_numeric ~ Groupe)
pairwise_week8 <- pairwise_wilcox_test(
  week8_data,
  esr_numeric ~ Groupe,
  p.adjust.method = "BH"
)

print(kruskal_week8)
print(pairwise_week8)

```

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(dplyr)
library(stats)

# --- 0. Data Preparation ---

# Assuming Blood_data_esr_clean is the cleaned data frame from the previous plot step.
Blood_data_esr_clean <- Blood_data_esr_clean %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Apply Rank Transformation
    esr_rank = rank(esr_numeric), 
    
    # 2. Ensure factors are defined (essential for the model formula)
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Control", "Winter", "Summer")),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id) # Assuming Shark_Id exists in the data
  )

# --- 1. Fit the Models (REML=FALSE) ---

# Model B (Full): Includes Sex Factor
model_esr_full <- lmer(
  esr_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_data_esr_clean, 
  REML = FALSE
) 

# Model A (Reduced): Excludes Sex Factor
model_esr_reduced <- lmer(
  esr_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_data_esr_clean, 
  REML = FALSE
)

# --- 2. Compile and Print Results ---

print("--- ESR (Rank) Model Comparison ---")

results_esr <- data.frame(
  Variable = "ESR",
  Model = c("Full (with Sex)", "Reduced (without Sex)"),
  AIC = c(AIC(model_esr_full), AIC(model_esr_reduced)),
  BIC = c(BIC(model_esr_full), BIC(model_esr_reduced)),
  LogLik = c(logLik(model_esr_full), logLik(model_esr_reduced)),
  df = c(attr(logLik(model_esr_full), "df"), attr(logLik(model_esr_reduced), "df"))
)

print(results_esr)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Make sure 'esr' is numeric
Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  mutate(esr = as.numeric(as.character(esr)))

# Mean ± SD of esr by Group
esr_group_summary <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  group_by(Groupe_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(esr, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(esr, na.rm = TRUE),
    n    = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

esr_group_summary

library(dplyr)

# Summary for Summer group only, by week
esr_summer_summary <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  filter(Groupe == "Summer") %>%
  group_by(week) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(esr, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(esr, na.rm = TRUE),
    n    = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

esr_summer_summary
```

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# ----------------------------
# 0) Prepare data & factors
# ----------------------------
Blood_count$Groupe[Blood_count$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Static"

Blood_data_clean <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    week_factor = factor(week, levels = c("4", "6", "8")),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Winter", "Static", "Summer")),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(esr))  # keep only ESR values

Blood_data_clean <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  mutate(esr = as.numeric(as.character(esr)))

group_colors <- c("Winter" = "#FFF5E6", "Static" = "#FDB888", "Summer" = "#B30000")

# ----------------------------
# 1) Fit LMMs
# ----------------------------
model_esr_full <- lmer(
  esr ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_data_clean,
  REML = FALSE
)

model_esr_reduced <- lmer(
  esr ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_data_clean,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(model_esr_full, model_esr_reduced)

# ----------------------------
# 2) Pairwise comparisons (CLD letters)
# ----------------------------
emm_groups_week <- emmeans(model_esr_full, ~ Groupe_factor | week_factor)
letters_week <- cld(emm_groups_week, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% mutate(.group = gsub(" ", "", .group))

emm_weeks_group <- emmeans(model_esr_full, ~ week_factor | Groupe_factor)
letters_weeks <- cld(emm_weeks_group, Letters = LETTERS, adjust = "tukey") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% mutate(.group = gsub(" ", "", .group))

# ----------------------------
# 3) Boxplot stats for positioning letters
# ----------------------------
box_stats <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  group_by(week_factor, Groupe_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    ymin   = min(esr, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(esr, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    middle = median(esr, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(esr, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    ymax   = max(esr, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

letters_week <- left_join(letters_week, box_stats, by = c("week_factor", "Groupe_factor")) %>%
  mutate(
    y_pos = ymax + 0.05*(ymax - ymin),
    x_nudge = ifelse(Groupe_factor == "Summer" & week_factor == "4", -0.01, 0)
  )

letters_weeks <- left_join(letters_weeks, box_stats, by = c("week_factor", "Groupe_factor")) %>%
  mutate(
    y_pos = ymax + 0.17*(ymax - ymin),
    x_nudge = ifelse(Groupe_factor == "Summer" & week_factor == "4", -0.01, 0)
  )

letters_week <- letters_week %>%
  mutate(.group = .group)  # no change

letters_weeks <- letters_weeks %>%
  mutate(
    .group = case_when(
      Groupe_factor == "Summer" & week_factor == "4" ~ "A",
      Groupe_factor == "Summer" & week_factor %in% c("6", "8") ~ "B",
      TRUE ~ .group  # keep other letters unchanged
    )
  )

# ----------------------------
# 4) Plot ESR
# ----------------------------
p_esr <- ggplot(Blood_data_clean, aes(x = week_factor, y = esr, fill = Groupe_factor)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Groupe_factor),
             shape = 21, size = 2, stroke = 0.5,
             position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.8) +
  # lowercase letters
  geom_text(data = letters_week,
            aes(x = as.numeric(week_factor) + x_nudge, y = y_pos, label = .group, group = Groupe_factor),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
            vjust = 0, size = 6) +
  # uppercase letters
  geom_text(data = letters_weeks,
            aes(x = as.numeric(week_factor) + x_nudge, y = y_pos, label = .group, group = Groupe_factor),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
            vjust = 0, size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Winter"="black", "Static"="black", "Summer"="black"), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = "", y = "ESR (mm/h)", fill = "Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 17),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 17),
    axis.line = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20)
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Week 4", "Week 6", "Week 8")) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")

print(p_esr)

# ----------------------------
# 5) Export
# ----------------------------
ggsave("Figure_ESR.png", p_esr, width = 15, height = 12, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Figure_ESR.pdf", p_esr, width = 15, height = 12)

```







```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(dplyr)
library(stats)

# --- 0. Data Preparation ---

# Assuming Blood_data_esr_clean is the cleaned data frame.
Blood_data_esr_clean <- Blood_data_esr_clean %>%
  mutate(
    # Ensure raw numeric variable and factors are defined
    esr_numeric = as.numeric(as.character(esr)), 
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Control", "Winter", "Summer")),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id) 
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(esr_numeric)) # Filter out NAs used in the plotting step

# --- 1. Fit the LMM (REML=TRUE) ---

# CRITICAL: Using the raw esr_numeric data as the outcome
lmm_esr_raw <- lmer(
  esr_numeric ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_data_esr_clean, 
  REML = TRUE
) 

# --- 2. Print the Model Summary ---

print("--- Summary of LMM for RAW ESR Data ---")
summary(lmm_esr_raw)



library(emmeans)
library(DHARMa)
library(writexl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(emmeans)
library(writexl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# --- 1. Post-hoc comparisons (Week vs. Week within Group) ---

print("\n--- Post-hoc Week vs. Week Comparisons (Bonferroni-adjusted) ---")

# Calculate EMMs for Week factor, conditioned by Group factor
emms_esr_week_by_group <- emmeans(
  lmm_esr_raw, 
  specs = ~ week_factor | Groupe_factor
)

print(lmm_esr_raw)
summary(lmm_esr_raw)
anova(lmm_esr_raw)
# Perform pairwise comparisons using Bonferroni adjustment
pairs_esr_week_by_group <- pairs(
  emms_esr_week_by_group, 
  adjust = "bonferroni"
) |> summary()

# --- Convert to data frame and export ---
pairs_esr_week_by_group_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_esr_week_by_group)

# Clean up and select columns for export
pairs_esr_week_by_group_df_export <- pairs_esr_week_by_group_df %>%
  select(Groupe_factor, contrast, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value) %>%
  rename(`P-Value` = p.value)

print(pairs_esr_week_by_group_df_export)

# --- 2. Export to Excel ---
write_xlsx(pairs_esr_week_by_group_df_export, "ESR_Raw_Week_by_Week_Comparisons.xlsx")

print("\nSuccessfully exported: ESR_Raw_Week_by_Week_Comparisons.xlsx")

```
```{r}

```
